channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.content:encoded: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: New security system causes major delays at Europe's airports amid peak travel season
item.description: The European Union recently implemented a new security system to better monitor foreigners who enter and exit. But its messy rollout has upended the summer travel season.
item.content:encoded: The European Union recently implemented a new security system to better monitor foreigners who enter and exit. But its messy rollout has upended the summer travel season.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: President Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over. What happens next?
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Ariane Tabatabai, Vice President of Research, Security and Defense, at the Chicago Council on Global Affairs, about developments in the war between the U.S. and Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Ariane Tabatabai, Vice President of Research, Security and Defense, at the Chicago Council on Global Affairs, about developments in the war between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: This English professor has run with the bulls in Spain for two decades
item.description: When Bill Hillmann was 19 years old, he read Ernest Hemingway's The Sun Also Rises. That book inspired him to pursue two dreams: a career in literature and to run with the bulls in Pamplona, Spain.
item.content:encoded: When Bill Hillmann was 19 years old, he read Ernest Hemingway's The Sun Also Rises. That book inspired him to pursue two dreams: a career in literature and to run with the bulls in Pamplona, Spain.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Are we living through the end of reading?
item.description: People are reading fewer and fewer books. The Atlantic's Rose Horowitch discusses what a post-literate world might look like.
item.content:encoded: People are reading fewer and fewer books. The Atlantic's Rose Horowitch discusses what a post-literate world might look like.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Old Crow Medicine Show's new album is a 'love letter' to America
item.description: Ketch Secor of the band, Old Crow Medicine Show, says his group's latest album, Union Made, is a love letter to the United States. It's full of stories from the country's past and present.
item.content:encoded: Ketch Secor of the band, Old Crow Medicine Show, says his group's latest album, Union Made, is a love letter to the United States. It's full of stories from the country's past and present.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: A veteran foreign correspondent looks back on a career covering conflicts
item.description: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.
item.content:encoded: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
item.content:encoded:
The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The stakes of a peace deal for Iran
item.description: Iran remains defiant against U.S., but stands to rake in billions if a peace deal can be reached.
item.content:encoded: Iran remains defiant against U.S., but stands to rake in billions if a peace deal can be reached.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: On the streets of Paris, hearts were divided as France beat Morocco in the World Cup
item.description: France beat Morocco to advance to the World Cup semifinals. The match drew crowds to Paris' streets to watch on big screens. In a country with a large Moroccan diaspora, many hearts were divided.
item.content:encoded: France beat Morocco to advance to the World Cup semifinals. The match drew crowds to Paris' streets to watch on big screens. In a country with a large Moroccan diaspora, many hearts were divided.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley
